corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: vocative call, bridge, attention particle, and following addressee must be certified before live repair
what_failed: The hover collapsed the vocative formula into a phrase-like gloss and did not teach the token pieces.
sarf_lesson: Segment only real vocative pieces; keep lexical yā stems out of this lane.
nahw_lesson: The call particle, bridge, attention particle, and addressee are separate roles in the address construction.
learner_explanation: يَٰٓأَيُّهَا is not just 'you'; it is an address formula whose pieces lead into the following addressee.
drill_prompt: Break يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ into call, bridge, attention particle, and addressee, then decide what the hover may show.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: preserve the vocative call and addressee/possessive structure before repair
what_failed: The visible addressee host was populated while the call/address contribution was not learner-visible.
sarf_lesson: Do not mistake this for a bare lexical host; identify the fused address shape before glossing.
nahw_lesson: A munādā/addressee relation changes the learner explanation even when the English phrase is familiar.
learner_explanation: The token addresses the addressee; the hover must not reduce it to the noun alone.
drill_prompt: Explain why يَٰبَنِىٓ is more than 'sons/children' and name the address structure.

visible_bad_hover: dry (hover not defective; detector falsely routed the row as vocative)
corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: no live hover repair; classify as not_vocative_surface_prefix and keep the lexical host intact
what_failed: The review detector treated a yā-initial lexical surface as if it had a vocative particle.
sarf_lesson: Raw starts-with matching is not segmentation; prove a real clitic before splitting.
nahw_lesson: Nahw should only review vocative structure after sarf proves a call particle or address form.
learner_explanation: In يَابِسٍ the yā belongs to the word 'dry'; it is not the particle 'O'.
drill_prompt: Compare يَابِسٍ and يَٰقَوْمِ; identify which has a vocative particle and why.
